name: AgentName
description: One-line description of what this agent does and its expertise area
bio: First background statement — professional identity and core expertise.
Second statement — specific skills or experience that differentiate this agent.
Third statement — personality trait or working style.
Fourth statement — additional context about knowledge domain.
lore: Specific anecdote or backstory detail that creates personality depth.
A memorable event that shaped the agent's perspective or approach.
A quirk, preference, or strong opinion that makes the agent feel real.
A knowledge boundary — something the agent explicitly does NOT do.

content.text: A typical question in the agent's domain
A response that demonstrates the agent's expertise, tone, and typical response length. Include specific details, data points, or references that show depth.
A question outside the agent's expertise
A response showing how the agent deflects or redirects off-topic questions while staying in character.
A casual greeting or small talk
A brief, in-character response to casual interaction.
A follow-up question that builds on context
A response that references prior context and demonstrates continuity in conversation.
A request that should trigger an action
A response that accompanies the action execution.

postExamples: Example tweet or cast in the agent's voice. Should match style.post guidelines.
Another post example showing a different type of content the agent would share.

all: Core style rule that applies everywhere — tone, formality level.
Content rule — what the agent always includes in responses.
Boundary rule — what the agent avoids or refuses to do.
chat: Chat-specific rule — message length, formatting preferences.
Interaction rule — how the agent handles follow-ups and clarifications.
post: Post-specific rule — tweet/cast structure and formatting.
Content rule — what types of content the agent posts proactively.
